summary: User policy for the Aero Shake gesture. The local Microsoft Desktop.admx file explicitly documents the same Explorer policy key, NoWindowMinimizingShortcuts value name, and 1/0 mapping that the app uses.

setting.name: Aero Shake Window Minimizing Gesture
setting.casual_explanation: Aero Shake is the gesture where you grab a window and shake it to minimize or restore other windows. This policy decides whether that gesture works for the current user.
setting.why_people_change_it: People turn it off when they trigger it by accident or simply do not like gesture-based window management.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Disabling Aero Shake prevents accidental minimization or restore behavior, but it also removes a small desktop convenience feature for users who actually use it.
setting.professional_notes: This maps to the user-scoped NoWindowMinimizingShortcuts policy in Desktop.admx. Enabled policy state writes 1 and disabled policy state writes 0.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Aero Shake stays available because the user policy is not forcing it off.

allowed_values.label: Aero Shake allowed
allowed_values.meaning: The policy is disabled and the Aero Shake gesture remains available.

allowed_values.label: Aero Shake blocked
allowed_values.meaning: The policy is enabled and the Aero Shake gesture is turned off.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes NoWindowMinimizingShortcuts = 1 for the current user, which matches the documented disable behavior.

windows_defaults.label: Windows user-policy default

states.rationale: The user policy is not configured by default.

windows_defaults.notes: Not configured corresponds to the normal Aero Shake behavior remaining available.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Users who want standard desktop behavior
avoid_for: Users who often trigger Aero Shake by accident
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want to preserve the normal Windows desktop gesture set.
tradeoffs: No explicit user policy is enforced.

recommended_profiles.label: No accidental window shake
intended_for: Users who want a calmer desktop experience
avoid_for: Users who intentionally use Aero Shake

states.rationale: Turn the gesture off for the current user.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want to stop windows from minimizing or restoring due to the shake gesture.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You rely on the gesture as part of your workflow.

evidence.title: Microsoft ADMX_Desktop Policy CSP
evidence.summary: Official documentation for NoWindowMinimizingShortcuts and its user-scope mapping.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft Desktop.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file shows NoWindowMinimizingShortcuts with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0 under the Explorer policy key.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes NoWindowMinimizingShortcuts = 1 for the current user.

evidence.title: Existing tweak source record
evidence.summary: The repo already tracks provenance for this tweak in the current catalog output.

decision.why: The local Microsoft Desktop.admx file explicitly defines NoWindowMinimizingShortcuts under Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0, and the paired ADML help text states that enabling the policy prevents windows from being minimized or restored when the active window is shaken. The app writes that documented enabled-policy state.

validation_proof.notes: The local official Microsoft ADMX file names the same Explorer policy key and NoWindowMinimizingShortcuts value used by the app, and it confirms the 1/0 mapping. The paired ADML help text states that enabling the policy turns off the Aero Shake gesture.
